sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Real Betis have shown mixed form in their last 5 matches, with 3 wins, 1 draw, and 1 heavy loss to Real Madrid (5-1). Villarreal, on the other hand, have been more consistent, securing 3 wins in their last 5, though they suffered losses to Barcelona (0-2) and Real Racing Club (2-1). Villarreal's attacking output has been strong with over 2.5 goals in 4 of their last 5 games.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last 2 encounters, matches between Real Betis and Villarreal have been competitive, with a 2-2 draw in October 2025 and a 1-2 Villarreal win in April 2025. Both teams have scored in the last 6 head-to-head games, and over 2.5 goals have been recorded in all of their last 5 meetings.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Villarreal sit 3rd in the league with 41 points and an impressive 37 goals scored, showcasing their attacking prowess. Real Betis, in 6th with 29 points, have scored 31 goals but struggle defensively, conceding 25 compared to Villarreal's 17. Betis' high interception and clearance rates could help disrupt Villarreal's flow.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: For Betis, Aitor Ruibal has been in form with 2 goals and 2 assists in recent matches, while Villarreal's Alberto Moleiro stands out with 8 goals this season. Betis will miss key forward Cucho Hernández (8 goals), which could impact their attack, whereas Villarreal have no major absences.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Villarreal's superior league position, recent form, and Sofascore's 49% probability of an away win, they are the favorites. Betis' home advantage at Benito Villamarin and fighting spirit in recent head-to-heads could make it close, but Villarreal's attacking consistency might edge them out. Expect a high-scoring game based on historical trends.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, the scoreline remains goalless between Real Betis and Villarreal at the Benito Villamarin. Both teams have had their opportunities, with neither side able to convert their chances into goals. Real Betis have dominated possession and shown more presence in the final third, while Villarreal have created a couple of notable openings despite having less of the ball. The game is finely poised, with both teams showing intent but lacking the final touch in front of goal.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Real Betis first-half performance
halftimeSections.text: Real Betis have controlled much of the game through their superior ball possession and frequent entries into the opponent’s final third. They've managed several shots on target and created a significant chance, though they failed to capitalize on it. Their defensive structure has been relatively solid, limiting Villarreal's ability to penetrate consistently. Players like Antony and Pablo Fornals have been active in attack, while the backline, led by Natan, has held firm against Villarreal’s counters.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Villarreal first-half performance
halftimeSections.text: Villarreal, despite having less possession, have been dangerous on the break and created a couple of clear-cut opportunities, though they couldn’t convert. Their defensive efforts have been commendable, managing to frustrate Real Betis despite the hosts’ dominance. Alfonso Pedraza has stood out with his creative contributions, while Georges Mikautadze and Gerard Moreno have posed threats upfront. However, their lack of control in midfield has limited their overall influence on the game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating attacking momentum between the two sides. Real Betis started strongly with consistent pressure in the opening minutes, maintaining a sustained presence in Villarreal’s half. Villarreal responded with a sharp attacking spell midway through the half, pushing Betis back temporarily. However, Betis regained control later on, finishing the half with a series of threatening moves. These shifts indicate that while Betis have been more dominant overall, Villarreal remain a threat when given space to attack.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second-half expectations
halftimeSections.text: The second half is likely to see Real Betis continue their high-possession approach as they seek to break the deadlock. With their superior presence in the final third, they are expected to create more chances if they can sharpen their finishing. Villarreal, on the other hand, will likely rely on counter-attacks and set pieces to exploit any defensive lapses from the hosts. Both teams are expected to push for a goal, and tactical adjustments at half-time could play a significant role in determining the outcome. Given the chances created in the first half, it’s reasonable to expect at least a couple of goals in the second period.
